evidence.scientific_clarity: Force-directed spring layout with 4 community colors on ~30 nodes directly fulfills the brief; edge alpha=0.25 and node_size=220 avoid hairball.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0 set; matplotlib defaults to DejaVu Sans; node label font_size=6.5 is small but functional.
evidence.palette: Custom 4-color palette (red/blue/teal/yellow) is reasonably distinct and not directional, but is not a certified colorblind-safe set (e.g. ColorBrewer); red-teal pair may confuse deuteranopes.
evidence.layout_density: 7x7in figure, k=1.8 spreads nodes well, tight_layout pad=0.3, equal aspect ratio; no overlapping labels or gaping whitespace expected.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption correctly names colors and communities, legend is present in lower right, but no numerical claim (e.g. exact cross-community edge count or modularity score).
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is saved but script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42, so text is Type 3 (non-selectable bitmap) rather than editable vector.
evidence.file_format: All four required files present (figure.pdf, figure.png, caption.tex, gen_figure.py) plus bonus figure.svg; all non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains layout, node count, community colors, and edge opacity, but 'a small number of cross-community edges' is vague — a specific count would strengthen it.

overall_strengths: All required output files are present and a bonus SVG is included; the generation script is clean and self-contained.
Layout parameters (spring k=1.8, edge alpha=0.25, node_size=220) are well-tuned to avoid hairball and make communities visually separable.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype=0 means text in the PDF/SVG is not selectable or editable vector — a standard requirement for journal submission.
Caption lacks a specific numerical finding (e.g. exact cross-community edge count) and the palette is not a certified colorblind-safe set.
